description: Run an array of async task functions with a concurrency limit
prompt: Write a JavaScript ES module that exports an async function `promisePool(tasks, concurrency)` which takes an array of task functions (each returns a Promise) and a concurrency limit. It should execute tasks with at most `concurrency` tasks running simultaneously. Return an array of results in the same order as the input tasks. If any task rejects, the returned promise should reject with that error. Export as named export.

tests.name: resolves all tasks and returns results in order
tests.code: const tasks = [() => Promise.resolve('a'), () => Promise.resolve('b'), () => Promise.resolve('c')]; const result = await mod.promisePool(tasks, 2); assert(JSON.stringify(result) === JSON.stringify(['a', 'b', 'c']), 'Expected [a,b,c] but got ' + JSON.stringify(result));

tests.name: respects concurrency limit
tests.code: let active = 0; let maxActive = 0; const makeTask = (ms) => async () => { active++; if (active > maxActive) maxActive = active; await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, ms)); active--; return ms; }; const tasks = [makeTask(50), makeTask(50), makeTask(50), makeTask(50)]; await mod.promisePool(tasks, 2); assert(maxActive <= 2, 'Max concurrent was ' + maxActive + ', expected at most 2');

tests.name: handles empty task array
tests.code: const result = await mod.promisePool([], 3); assert(JSON.stringify(result) === '[]', 'Expected [] but got ' + JSON.stringify(result));

tests.name: rejects if a task rejects
tests.code: const tasks = [() => Promise.resolve(1), () => Promise.reject(new Error('boom')), () => Promise.resolve(3)]; try { await mod.promisePool(tasks, 2); assert(false, 'Should have thrown'); } catch (e) { assert(e.message === 'boom', 'Expected boom but got ' + e.message); }

tests.name: works with concurrency of 1 (sequential execution)
tests.code: const order = []; const tasks = [async () => { order.push(1); return 1; }, async () => { order.push(2); return 2; }, async () => { order.push(3); return 3; }]; const result = await mod.promisePool(tasks, 1); assert(JSON.stringify(order) === JSON.stringify([1, 2, 3]), 'Expected sequential order'); assert(JSON.stringify(result) === JSON.stringify([1, 2, 3]), 'Expected [1,2,3]');
